msg: While holding: arm a Cosmo monitor on Workstream WS-27 "PR cleanup" (page 38e8bce9-1f7c-80c7-b212-c6a1d258966b) filtered on the Workstream relation, watching each WI's Stage for review verdicts — Reviewing->Closed = approved (advance), Reviewing->Executing = rejected/rework (re-claim, read the reviewer note, re-dispatch, re-complete). A GLOBAL reviewer session already covers MentoMate workstreams incl. WS-27, so do NOT wire/own a reviewer — only your own read-side verdict monitor. Keep it in your monitor manifest; reconcile after restart. This is orientation, not residue work — stay in prime-and-hold until the release directive.

RELEASED from prime-and-hold. Orchestrator merged + finalized the 6 clean-green PRs to Stage=Reviewing (WI-885/480/560/1070/481/1075) — do NOT touch those; if the global reviewer bounces one to Executing, that bounce is your rework. Active residue (re-derive live state vs current main, which now includes the 6 merges): DIRTY conflicts — WI-1119/PR1604 (raw nav-contract usage + missing accessibilityViewIsModal), WI-902/PR1618 (mobile manual-plural-guard), WI-1151/PR1644 (alias-merge integration tests; NOT redundant vs #1638, zero file overlap). Residual reds — WI-1059/PR1617 (SessionSummaryScreen testIDs + main test), WI-1132/PR1643 (Merge completeness check). Draft — WI-1071/PR1595 (restore dropped _wip/umbrella-program/single-wi-executor-protocol.md, re-verify Merge completeness, mark ready). HOLD, do NOT start: WI-1087/PR1593 + WI-1097/PR1609 — CI-green but CHANGES_REQUESTED automated review (1593: redefines a @eduagent/schemas type = blocking; 1609: missing GDPR-export regression = should-fix); awaiting operator fix-vs-override ruling. Per WI: take over the stale crowka claim, dispatch a typed builder, merge only on strict-green, finalize via /cosmo:execute complete.

Operator ruling: route WI-1087/PR1593 and WI-1097/PR1609 to FIX (do NOT override the review). Both now in your active queue. WI-1087/PR1593 — review BLOCKING: the service redefines an API-facing type that already exists in @eduagent/schemas (AGENTS.md non-negotiable — @eduagent/schemas is the shared contract; never redefine locally). Fix: delete the local type, import the canonical schema-package type, reconcile usages + tests. WI-1097/PR1609 — review SHOULD_FIX: the export-v2.ts change drops data silently in the GDPR export with no regression coverage. Fix: add a regression test that fails on the silent drop, then confirm corrected behavior. Read each PR's full automated-review comment for specifics. Per WI: take over the stale crowka claim, dispatch a builder, drive the review to green, merge on strict-green, finalize via /cosmo:execute complete.

UPDATE (supersedes inbox-2 for the satellites): the two bug-tracker PRs are now Cosmo WIs in WS-27, Stage=Ready, EP=Assisted — execute as normal lane work. WI-1165 mirrors PR #1603 (Harden Inngest failure cleanup): PR is DIRTY (conflicts with the merged @inngest-admin sweep, WI-1075) — resolve conflict, finish to strict-green, clear review findings, merge, /cosmo:execute complete. WI-1166 mirrors PR #1613 (account reclaim): PR is CLEAN, automated review CHANGES_REQUESTED but the gc1-allow blocker is MOOT (API Quality Gate passed) — add the Resend retry-path test, drop the unused accounts import, normalize the gc1-allow escape, then merge + complete. Both: AC + scope note on each WI page (confirm PR coverage vs the full issue); take over the stale crowka claim on the PR branch. Bug-tracker close is ORCHESTRATOR-owned — do NOT touch the Open-tracker issues; after the reviewer closes each WI, the orchestrator closes the mirrored bug item. These join your active queue with 1593/1609 + the DIRTY/red residue.

ack pr-cleanup-1. Confirmed root cause — the green-sweep merge order created this (WI-885's new billing-subscription-store-teardown.ts merged before WI-1075's @inngest-admin guard; strict=false so no up-to-date gate). Inline 1-line annotation is the correct resolution, NOT a separate WI — proceed, land via your normal Gate-1 PR. One caveat before you commit the scope value: match the @inngest-admin scope to what the function actually touches in the DB, not just to account-deletion.ts's annotation — a whole-org erasure teardown may be cross-profile rather than event-profile. Acknowledged as your decision; no orchestrator action needed. Nice catch — this would have blocked the whole residue.

ack pr-cleanup-2 — no-db is right, and good catch de-duping #1132's annotation. One throughput advisory (your call): main is RED right now and that blocks the `main` check on EVERY API PR in the residue. #1593 (WI-1087) carries a BLOCKING schema-dup review finding, so it is not minutes-from-merge — bundling the unblock into it keeps the whole residue stalled until #1593 is fixed + green + merged. Unless #1593's schema-dup fix is genuinely imminent, prefer a STANDALONE 1-line hotfix PR for the no-db annotation: it clears required checks in ~5-15 min and unblocks all residue CI now, decoupled from #1593's review cycle. Advisory, not an order — you have the live picture.

Heads-up on WI-1151 (#1644): your builder resolved the conflict — PR #1644 is now CLEAN — but the WI is stranded at Stage=Captured (EP=Unset, unclaimed; crowka never claimed it). Same lifecycle gap as the mirrored satellites: a Captured item cannot be claimed or finalized — /cosmo:refine rejects non-Backlog, and /cosmo:execute complete needs it Executing. Before you merge #1644: run /cosmo:triage (Captured->Backlog) -> /cosmo:refine --to-ready (AC is already set; just confirm + classify EP=Assisted) -> claim (Executing). Then merge + complete. Otherwise the WI skips the lifecycle and review will bounce it. Do NOT merge #1644 until WI-1151 is Executing.
